ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANTS PAUL H. MELBOSTAD AND HARRIS & MCSPARRAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
LAW AND MOTION 302, DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT CONTINUED FROM MAR-27-2012 TO LAW AND MOTION AT APR-10-2012, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 PER AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PAUL MELBOSTAD AND GOLDSTEIN, GELLMAN, MELBOSTAD, HARRIS & MCSPARRRAN, LLP'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION. ALL CAUSES OF ACTION ESSENTIALLY ALLEGE PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE, WHICH IS TIME BARRED UNDER CCP 340.6. AMENDMENT WILL NOT CURE THIS DEFECT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

TRIAL MOTION IN DEPT. 220, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE COURT'S ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEFENDANT'S OPPOSITION IS DENIED. JUDGE JAMES J. MCBRIDE, CLERK TRINA CLARK,NOT REPORTED (220).

Off Calendar May-16-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF REGULO SIERRA'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION.
No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Because the motion seeks reconsideration of an order issued by Judge McBride, the motion will need to be heard by Judge McBride, who is assigned to department 220. The parties should contact Judge McBride's courtroom clerk at 551-3771 to arrange for a date and time for a hearing. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-10-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Paul H. Melbostad, Esq., and Alan N. Herzfeld, Esq., from Goldstein, Gellman, Melbostad, Harris & McSparran LLP, 415-673-5600, for defendants and moving party
Regulo Sierra, 415-672-3637, plaintiff and opposing party in Propia Persona

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling:
DEFENDANTS PAUL MELBOSTAD AND GOLDSTEIN, GELLMAN, MELBOSTAD, HARRIS & MCSPARRRAN, LLP'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to all causes of action. All causes of action essentially allege professional negligence, which is time barred under CCP 340.6. Amendment will not cure this defect. Order signed in open court. Judge: James J. McBride; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

A R G U E D. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs PAUL MELBOSTAD, GOLDSTEIN, GELLMAN, MELBOSTAD and HARRIS & MCSPARRAN's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff is granted leave, if he can do so in good faith, to plead facts showing that his claim is timely under Code of Civil Procedure 340.6. Order signed in open Court.
